items.q: Quelles langues sont prises en charge ?
items.a: Anglais, japonais, coréen, chinois, espagnol, français et allemand. De nouvelles langues sont ajoutées chaque mois.

La plupart des apprenants constatent une différence nette en 4 à 6 semaines s'ils maintiennent la routine de 10 minutes. Le système de streak et la répétition espacée sont conçus pour faire tenir l'habitude.
Oui. Le site est entièrement responsive et fonctionne très bien sur les navigateurs mobiles. Ajoutez-le à votre écran d'accueil pour une expérience proche d'une appli.

faq.seoDescription: Réponses aux questions fréquentes sur l'apprentissage par répétition espaciée LangOria : fonctionnement de la boucle 10 minutes, prix, langues prises en charge et compte.
faq.title: Questions fréquentes
faq.subtitle: Tout ce que vous devez savoir sur LangOria.
faq.intro: Vous ne trouvez pas la réponse ? Écrivez-nous via la communauté, notre équipe vous répondra.

items.a: Cliquez sur 'S'inscrire' en haut à droite, saisissez email et mot de passe, choisissez langue cible et langue natale, et c'est parti.
Non. L'expérience d'apprentissage est gratuite. Il vous faudra un moyen de paiement uniquement si vous passez à Premium plus tard.

items.q: Puis-je changer ma langue cible plus tard ?
items.a: Oui. Allez dans Paramètres → Langue cible à tout moment. Votre progression reste liée à la langue que vous pratiquiez.

items.q: Combien de temps étudier chaque jour ?
items.a: Nous recommandons 10 minutes par jour. La répétition espaciée fonctionne mieux avec des sessions courtes et régulières.

items.q: Pourquoi les explications sont-elles dans ma langue natale ?
items.a: Les recherches montrent qu'apprendre est plus rapide quand les explications viennent de votre langue la plus forte. Les mots étudiés restent toujours dans la langue cible.

items.q: Mon streak est-il réinitialisé si je saute un jour ?
items.a: Oui, un jour complet sans apprentissage réinitialise le streak. Une fenêtre de grâce de 36 h couvre les décalages horaires.
Vos données d'apprentissage sont stockées sur Supabase (Postgres) dans une région proche de vous. Nous ne vendons aucune donnée utilisateur.
Allez dans Profil → Zone de danger et cliquez 'Supprimer le compte'. Toutes les données sont effacées sous 7 jours.

posts.title: Why 10 minutes a day beats 2 hours on weekends
posts.excerpt: Spaced repetition is the most researched technique in learning science. Here is why shorter, more frequent sessions outperform long cramming.
content: If you have ever crammed for an exam and forgotten everything two weeks later, you already know the problem with long, infrequent study sessions. The fix is not studying harder. The fix is studying more often.

Learning science has been clear on this for decades. Hermann Ebbinghaus discovered the forgetting curve in 1885: we lose newly learned information at a predictable rate unless we actively recall it at the right moment. The 'right moment' is just before you are about to forget.

This is exactly what spaced repetition does. LangOria schedules each word and grammar point for review at the moment you are most likely to forget it. Hit 'remembered' and the next review gets pushed further out. Hit 'not familiar' and the next review comes sooner.

The result: in 10 minutes a day, you cover more ground than 2 hours on Sunday, because every minute is spent on something your brain actually needs to recall. After 30 days, most learners have reviewed 600+ items — more than a single weekend cramming session could ever cover.
So if you have been telling yourself you don't have time to learn a language, you do. Ten minutes. Every day. That is the entire trick.

posts.title: How to remember 1000 words without burning out
posts.excerpt: A practical 8-week plan built on the 3-2-1 rule, with examples across English, Japanese, and Spanish.
content: Most language learners aim for 1000 words as a first milestone. The bad news: trying to memorize them all at once is a recipe for burnout. The good news: you do not need to memorize them. You need to meet them.
We use a simple 3-2-1 rule. Three encounters a day, two contexts per encounter, and one forced recall. That is it.

Day 1: learn 3 new words. See them in your flashcard, see them in a sentence, see them in a grammar example. Day 2: 3 more new words, plus 6 from yesterday. Day 7: 21 new words this week, but 80+ reviews. By the time a word has been reviewed 5 times, retention is over 90%.

Across 8 weeks, this adds up to 168 words at a pace of 3 per day. To hit 1000, you scale to 15 new words a day by month 3, when reviews are mostly cached. The 10-minute daily loop in LangOria handles this for you automatically.
The key insight: vocabulary is not a memory contest. It is a frequency game. Show up every day, trust the system, and the words will stick.

posts.title: Shadowing: the speaking exercise that actually works
posts.excerpt: Why repeating native audio is the fastest way to fix pronunciation, and a 5-step routine you can start today.
content: If you can read a sentence but cannot say it, you are not alone. Most learners spend 90% of their time reading and listening, and 10% speaking. That ratio is backwards.

Shadowing is the practice of listening to a native speaker and repeating what they say in real time, mimicking rhythm, intonation, and stress. It is the technique professional interpreters use to maintain fluency, and it works for any language.

The 5-step routine: (1) Listen to a sentence at native speed. (2) Listen again with the transcript. (3) Repeat at native speed while recording. (4) Compare your recording to the original. (5) Repeat 3 times before moving on.
Do this for 5 minutes a day. Within a month, your accent will start to shift noticeably. Within three months, you will be able to produce sentences you could not read aloud before.
LangOria's Speaking module is built around this exact idea. Pick a sentence, listen, repeat, repeat, repeat. No judging, no feedback anxiety. Just your voice and the model.

placement.resultSeoTitle: Résultat du test de niveau — LangOria
placement.noCourseMatch: Aucun cours ne correspond exactement à {{level}}. Rendez-vous sur la liste des cours et choisissez un niveau proche.
placement.saved: Enregistré sur votre compte. Ce niveau sera automatiquement recommandé la prochaine fois que vous ouvrirez cette langue.

errors.noQuestions: Aucune question de test de niveau pour cette langue pour le moment. Veuillez choisir une autre langue.
errors.noStartQuestion: Impossible de sélectionner une question de départ.
errors.fetchFailed: Échec de la récupération des questions

lesson.loginToTrackProgress: Log in to track lesson progress.
lesson.noLessons: This course has no lessons configured yet.

league.title: Weekly Leaderboard
league.subtitle: Accumulate XP weekly, compete for Master tier · promotions and demotions settle every Monday
league.seoDescription: Compete in LangOria's weekly XP leaderboard. Climb from Bronze to Master across language leagues.
league.loginCardTitle: Log in to see your rank
league.loginCardDesc: After logging in you'll automatically join this week's season and be placed in a division based on your total XP.
league.promotionMsg: You're in the promotion zone! You'll move up a division when the season ends.
league.demotionMsg: You're in the demotion zone and may drop a division when the season ends. Keep going!
league.rules: Promotion/demotion rules: top 10% of each division promote, bottom 10% demote. Settlements run in bulk every Monday when the season ends.
league.leaderboardSubtitle: Sorted by weekly XP · up to 50 per page
league.emptyDivision: No players in this division yet this week — grind XP and be the first on the board!

errors.noSpeech: No speech detected. Please try again.
errors.microphoneNotAllowed: Please allow microphone access in your browser.
errors.network: Network error. Please check your connection and retry.
errors.cannotStart: Unable to start recording. Please retry.

pronunciation.unsupportedBrowser: Speech recognition is not supported in this browser. Please use Chrome, Edge, or Safari.
pronunciation.toneHint: Tip: this score is based on overall similarity; tone accuracy will be supported in a future version.

aiConversation.title: AI Conversation
aiConversation.loginPrompt: Log in to start AI conversations.
aiConversation.subtitle: Practice real-life dialogue with AI.

alphabet.title: Alphabet
alphabet.subtitle: Learn the writing system and sounds.
alphabet.seoDescription: Learn Hiragana, Katakana, Hangul, Pinyin, Jyutping, Thai and more. Tap a character to hear it.
alphabet.ttsDisclaimer: Voice quality varies by browser and OS.
alphabet.tapDetailToScore: Tap a card to practice shadowing
alphabet.ttsDisclaimerDetail: Pronunciation is generated by your browser's built-in text-to-speech. Results may vary by browser, operating system, and voice pack.

cefr.title: CEFR Self-Assessment
cefr.subtitle: Find your level with Can-Do statements.
cefr.seoDescription: Self-assess your language level from A1 to C2 with Can-Do statements. Complements the placement test.
cefr.introText: Check the Can-Do statements you can confidently do. We will infer your CEFR level automatically.
cefr.assessmentSeoDescription: {{language}} CEFR self-assessment — check what you can do
cefr.guestHint: Log in to save your self-assessment and get course recommendations.
cefr.selectAtLeastOne: Please select at least one statement to save.
cefr.manualOverrideHint: Pick the level that best matches your ability.
cefr.notePlaceholder: e.g. I am comfortable speaking but weak at writing.

crown.noCheckpoints: No checkpoints in this course yet.
crown.hint: Pass checkpoint lessons with {{threshold}}% or higher to earn crowns.

grammarMap.noData: No grammar data for this language.

lessonPlayer.lockedHint: Complete previous lessons to unlock this one.
lessonPlayer.completeHint: Complete the lesson to save progress.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.subtitle: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
notFound.suggestion: Check the URL for typos, or try one of these:
notFound.seoDescription: The page you're looking for doesn't exist on LangOria. Head back to the homepage to start learning languages.

reading.title: Reading
reading.subtitle: Graded reading with comprehension questions
reading.seoDescription: Graded reading passages with glossary and comprehension questions across English, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, and more.
reading.disclaimer: Passages are generated or curated for learners and may not reflect native-only content.
reading.emptyHint: Try another level or language later.

writing.title: Writing
writing.subtitle: Practice writing with instant feedback
writing.seoDescription: Essays, emails, summaries, stories and dialogues. Instant scoring on length, variety, and keywords.
